subtasks.title: Activate Your Space
subtasks.description: Stand up, stretch, and walk purposefully to your designated study spot. Grab a stimulating drink like iced coffee, sparkling water, or a flavored tea on your way.

subtasks.title: Launch & Load
subtasks.description: Open your laptop or textbook directly to the *first* specific topic or concept you plan to tackle. No browsing, no getting sidetracked – just open to the exact page, slide, or video.

subtasks.title: Concept Capture Sprint
subtasks.description: Dive into a single, high-interest concept for a focused burst. Actively read 2-3 pages, review a specific set of slides, or watch a stimulating 10-15 minute explanatory video related to a key exam topic. Focus on understanding, not perfection.

subtasks.title: Active Recall Blast
subtasks.description: Immediately after the sprint, spend a quick burst testing yourself. Create 3-5 rapid-fire flashcards from what you just learned, or try 1-2 quick practice questions without looking at notes. The goal is retrieval and engagement, not exhaustive accuracy.

subtasks.title: Victory Lap & Next Step
subtasks.description: Take 30 seconds to acknowledge your accomplishment! Then, quickly jot down *one* specific question you have, or *one* topic you want to start with in your *next* short study burst. Close your materials completely to signal the end of this session.
